questions.text: Let $f(x) = x^3 - 5x^2 + 2x + 8$. Use the remainder theorem to find the remainder when $f(x)$ is divided by $(x-3)$.
questions.explanation: By the remainder theorem the remainder equals $f(3) = 27 - 45 + 6 + 8 = -4$.

questions.text: Let $g(x) = 3x^3 + x^2 - 4x + 2$. Find the remainder when $g(x)$ is divided by $(x-1)$.
questions.explanation: By the remainder theorem the remainder equals $g(1) = 3 + 1 - 4 + 2 = 2$ (dividing directly gives quotient $3x^2+4x$ with remainder $2$, confirming this).

questions.text: Which of the following is a factor of $x^3 - 2x^2 - 5x + 6$?
questions.explanation: Testing $x=1$: $1 - 2 - 5 + 6 = 0$, so by the factor theorem $(x-1)$ is a factor. In fact $x^3-2x^2-5x+6 = (x-1)(x-3)(x+2)$; the other options give non-zero values ($x=-1 \to 8$, $x=2 \to -4$, $x=-3 \to -24$).

questions.text: Dividing $p(x) = x^3 + x^2 - 10x + 8$ by $(x-2)$ gives quotient $q(x)$. What is $q(x)$?
questions.explanation: $p(2) = 8+4-20+8 = 0$, so $(x-2)$ is an exact factor. Dividing gives $p(x) = (x-2)(x^2+3x-4)$. Check by expanding: $(x-2)(x^2+3x-4) = x^3+3x^2-4x-2x^2-6x+8 = x^3+x^2-10x+8$, which matches $p(x)$.

questions.text: The cubic $x^3 - 7x + 6 = 0$ has three integer roots. What is the largest root?
questions.explanation: Testing $x=1$: $1-7+6=0$, so $(x-1)$ is a factor; dividing gives $x^3-7x+6=(x-1)(x^2+x-6)=(x-1)(x+3)(x-2)$. The roots are $x=1,\,-3,\,2$, so the largest is $2$.

questions.text: Which of the following are factors of $x^3 - 3x^2 - 6x + 8$? Select all that apply.

questions.explanation: Factorise both parts: $x^2-1=(x-1)(x+1)$ and $x^2+4x+3=(x+1)(x+3)$. Cancelling the common factor $(x+1)$ gives $\dfrac{x-1}{x+3}$. Check with $x=5$: original $=\frac{24}{48}=\frac{1}{2}$; simplified $=\frac{4}{8}=\frac{1}{2}$. âś“

Multiplying both sides by $(x+1)(x+4)$ gives $x+10 \equiv A(x+4) + B(x+1)$. Substituting $x=-1$: $9 = A(3) \Rightarrow A=3$. (Substituting $x=-4$ gives $6=B(-3)\Rightarrow B=-2$, so the full split is $\frac{3}{x+1}-\frac{2}{x+4}$; check: $\frac{3(x+4)-2(x+1)}{(x+1)(x+4)}=\frac{3x+12-2x-2}{(x+1)(x+4)}=\frac{x+10}{(x+1)(x+4)}$ âś“.)

Multiplying both sides by $(x+1)(x+2)$ gives $5x+7 \equiv A(x+2) + B(x+1)$. Substituting $x=-2$: $-3 = B(-1) \Rightarrow B=3$. (Substituting $x=-1$ gives $2=A(1)\Rightarrow A=2$, so the full split is $\frac{2}{x+1}+\frac{3}{x+2}$; check: $\frac{2(x+2)+3(x+1)}{(x+1)(x+2)}=\frac{2x+4+3x+3}{(x+1)(x+2)}=\frac{5x+7}{(x+1)(x+2)}$ âś“.)

questions.text: State the name of the theorem that tells you: if $f(a)=0$ then $(x-a)$ is a factor of $f(x)$.
questions.explanation: This is the **factor theorem**: $f(a)=0 \iff (x-a)$ is a factor of $f(x)$.
